---
title: "About"
image: static/img/GeorgeWRice.jpg
about:
  template: jolla
  links:
    - icon: github
      text: Github
      href: https://github.com/rmflight/george_rice_polar_expedition
    - icon: mastodon
      text: Mastodon
      href: https://mastodon.social/@rmflight
    - icon: envelope
      text: Email
      href: mailto:rflight79@gmail.com
---

## George W Rice

In the summer of 1881, George W Rice, from Berwick, Nova Scotia, Canada, was hired as the photographer for the scientific expedition to Lady Franklin Bay.
Sadly, he passed away on April 9, 1884, at the age of 28, before the remaining party members were rescued.
After their rescue, George's diary was returned to his parents.
The Berwick Register published extracts of his diary in the late 1800's.
Those newspaper clippings are being reproduced here, for everyone else to read.

## Robert M Flight

I am Robert M Flight, a bioinformatics analyst at the Markey Cancer Center, University of Kentucky.
My involvement in reproducing the newspaper clippings is a little roundabout.
George W Rice's family in Berwick, Nova Scotia, Canada, are a far removed branch of my Grandfather's, Robert E Rice, family.
Robert's nephew (name currently escaping me), was very interested in Rice family history, and obtained a copy of the diary newspaper clippings.
My Grandfather Robert made three copies of the clippings, one for each of his children.
When my mother passed from early onset Alzheimer's, I was given her copy.

As far as I am aware, outside of being used as background for books about the Greely expedition, no one else has been able to read the contents of George's diary.
I feel like that should be changed.
Also, in many places, the copies of the newspaper are barely legible.
I figured this way, it's much easier for both myself and others to be able to read George's story.

In addition, the copies were not in order when I received them.
I don't know if the originals were out of order, or if my Grandfather got them out of order when making the copies.
So, I did a bunch of work to get them in order.
I'm looking forward to reading George's diary along with you.

This copy of diary extracts has definitely gotten me more interested in family history.
